paper_title: In Situ Experimental Study of Natural Diatomaceous Earth Slopes under Alternating Dry and Wet Conditions

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the water content distribution in diatomaceous earth slopes under different conditions, such as natural and artificial rainfall, to understand the impact of these conditions on water infiltration and retention.

steps.description: By reading the monitoring data of the automatic acquisition instrument, the monitored distribution pattern of the water contents in each section on the 1:2 slope and 1:1.5 slope are shown in Figure 9a,b, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: read water content data from monitoring instrument

steps.description: Heavy rain and artificial simulated rainfall both led to an abrupt increase in the water content at each measuring point.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze impact of rainfall on water content

methods.description: This method examines the horizontal displacement of diatomaceous earth slopes under dry-wet cycles and rainfall conditions to assess stability and potential slip.

steps.description: By reading the monitoring data of the automatic acquisition instrument, the monitored horizontal displacement distribution of each section on the two types of slopes is shown in Figure 10a,b.
steps.searchable_summary: read horizontal displacement data from monitoring instrument

steps.description: Analyze the monitored horizontal displacements at various depths and their changes during dry-wet cycles and after rainfall.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the lateral earth pressure on diatomaceous earth slopes to understand the effects of artificial rainfall and other factors on slope stability.

steps.description: By reading the monitoring data of the automatic acquisition instrument, the monitored distribution pattern of the lateral earth pressure on each section of the 1:2 slope and 1:1.5 slope is shown in Figure 11a.
steps.searchable_summary: read lateral earth pressure data from monitoring instrument

steps.description: Analyze the changes in lateral earth pressure during artificial rainfall simulation and other conditions.
